# UC-19.2.35 · OpenStack Cinder Backend Free Capacity and Over-Subscription Risk

## Description

Cinder capacity is often thin-provisioned at the array. Low `free_gb` on a backend blocks new volume attaches and can surprise operators when snapshots inflate consumption.

## Value

Aligns OpenStack quota discussions with physical free space so tenant growth does not outpace array headroom.

## Implementation

Export scheduler pool stats or parse `cinder-volume` capacity update logs. Normalize `backend` names to match service catalog. Combine free-space alerts with array vendor telemetry where available. Document expected over-subscription ratio per storage class.

## SPL

```spl
index=openstack sourcetype="openstack:cinder" earliest=-4h
| eval free_pct=100*(free_gb/total_gb)
| eval thin_risk=if(provisioned_gb>(free_gb*2), 1, 0)
| where free_pct<=15 OR thin_risk=1
| stats latest(free_gb) as free, latest(total_gb) as total, latest(provisioned_gb) as prov by backend
| sort free
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (free GB by backend), Table (backends under threshold), Timechart (free_pct).

## References

- [OpenStack Cinder — Capacity planning](https://docs.openstack.org/cinder/latest/admin/capacity-planning.html)
